paper_title: Chemical and Aggregate Analysis in Marine Sediments

methods.description: This method describes the process of determining nitrate, nitrite, and ammonium concentrations using various chemical reduction and detection techniques, as well as analyzing nitrogen isotopic composition and C:N ratio of biomass.

steps.description: Nitrate and nitrite concentrations were determined on a chemiluminescence detector after being reduced to NO by VCl3 and NaI, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine nitrate and nitrite concentrations using chemiluminescence detection after chemical reduction.

steps.description: Ammonium concentration was measured with the salicylate method.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure ammonium concentration using the salicylate method.

steps.description: The 15N-labeled N2 was analyzed in the headspace of Exetainers on a gas chromatography-isotopic ratio mass spectrometer with the excess above natural abundance calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze 15N-labeled N2 using GC-IRMS and calculate excess above natural abundance.

steps.description: 15N-labeled NO3, NO2, and NH4 were analyzed after conversion to N2 via various assays.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert and analyze 15N-labeled NO3, NO2, and NH4 to N2 using specific assays.

steps.description: Nitrogen isotopic composition and C:N ratio of aggregate biomass were determined after washing and drying procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine nitrogen isotopic composition and C:N ratio of biomass after washing and drying.

methods.description: This method outlines the production of phycodetrital aggregates using cultured diatoms and the collection and preparation of coastal sediment samples for experimental use.

steps.description: Skeletonema marinoi was cultured using F/2 medium plus silicate to produce phycodetrital aggregates.
steps.searchable_summary: Culture Skeletonema marinoi using F/2 medium plus silicate to produce aggregates.

steps.description: Diatom aggregates were formed in bottles with stationary-phase culture and seawater, rotated on a plankton wheel.
steps.searchable_summary: Form diatom aggregates in bottles with culture and seawater, rotate on plankton wheel.

steps.description: Seawater in bottles was replaced at intervals to maintain oxygen levels and prevent waste accumulation.
steps.searchable_summary: Replace seawater in bottles to maintain oxygen levels and prevent waste accumulation.

steps.description: Coastal sediment was collected using an HAPS corer and subsampled with Plexiglas core liners.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect coastal sediment using HAPS corer and subsample with Plexiglas core liners.

steps.description: Sediment cores were prepared by removing macrofauna and transferring to a tank with artificial seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare sediment cores by removing macrofauna and transferring to tank with artificial seawater.
